title: [Bug]: PR label sync should ignore issue placeholders in Markdown code spans
summary: The pull request label synchronization helper scanned the default PR body and treated the instructional Markdown code-span placeholder Related #123 as a real source issue reference.
observed: Refreshing the issue branch PR copied labels from issue #209 and the placeholder issue #123 because the generated PR body contained the instructional text Use Related #123 in a code span.
expected: The helper should ignore issue-like text inside Markdown inline code spans and fenced code blocks, and the generated PR body should avoid literal placeholder issue numbers where possible.
reproduction: Run scripts/open-release-pr.sh on a PR body that contains an instructional Markdown code span with a placeholder issue reference and label synchronization enabled.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test proving that detect_source_issues ignores issue references inside Markdown code spans while still honoring real Related #... body references.
impact: A pull request can receive unrelated labels, making review and release filtering less precise. Runtime package behavior, delivery semantics, sink writes, and ACK ordering are unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This defect is limited to GitHub workflow metadata automation and cannot change message delivery semantics.
security: The fix must avoid copying unintended labels from unrelated issues and must continue to avoid secrets, private locators, payloads, or sensitive metadata in public comments and PR labels.
acceptance: Issue references inside Markdown inline code spans are ignored.
Issue references inside fenced code blocks are ignored.
Real non-code issue references in the PR body are still detected.
The default PR body avoids a literal #123 placeholder.
Focused unit tests cover the corrected behavior.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py tests/unit/test_pr_auto_approval.py -q and scripts/check.sh after the fix.
documentation: No additional public documentation is required. Keep CHANGELOG.md and the sanitized test report accurate.
closeout: Close after the bug branch has merged back into the feature branch and PR labels have been corrected on the live PR.
